sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: J.B. Hunt Transport Services, Inc. Declares Quarterly Dividend, Payable on May 22, 2026
feed.summary: J.B. Hunt Transport Services, Inc. announced a regular quarterly dividend of $0.45 per common share. This dividend will be payable on May 22, 2026, to stockholders of record as of May 8, 2026. The announcement was made on April 23, 2026, at 03:50 pm EDT.

feed.title: Carrier Celebrates Earth Day with Planting 1 Million Trees
feed.summary: Carrier Global Corporation announced its progress toward planting 5 million trees by 2030, having funded over 1 million trees in 2025 across various high-impact regions globally. This initiative, in collaboration with the Arbor Day Foundation, aims to enhance air quality, revive habitats, and improve climate resilience. Carrier plans to accelerate these efforts in 2026 by supporting more reforestation projects and encouraging stakeholder participation.

feed.title: Verisign Inc. (VRSN) Tops Q1 EPS by 3c
feed.summary: Verisign Inc. (VRSN) reported strong first-quarter results, exceeding analyst expectations for both earnings per share and revenue. The company posted Q1 EPS of $2.34, beating the estimate by $0.03, and achieved revenue of $429 million, surpassing the consensus forecast of $425.16 million.

feed.title: [424B2] GOLDMAN SACHS GROUP INC Prospectus Supplement
feed.summary: GS Finance Corp., guaranteed by The Goldman Sachs Group, Inc., is offering autocallable, contingent-coupon, index-linked notes tied to the S&P 500, Russell 2000, and Nasdaq-100 indices. These notes offer a potential monthly coupon of $6.042 per $1,000 if each index is at or above 70% of its initial level, with maturity expected on May 2, 2029, unless automatically called. Investors face significant risks, including potential loss of a substantial portion of their investment if the lesser performing underlier falls below its buffer level, and the tax treatment is considered uncertain due to potential IRS alternative characterizations.

feed.title: 32 large-load customers: why Georgia Power wants up to 6,000 MW more
feed.summary: Georgia Power has filed a request with the Georgia Public Service Commission (PSC) to procure 2,000 to 6,000 MW of new dispatchable capacity by 2033, including thermal, storage, and renewables, to meet the growing energy demands of the state. The company also seeks to certify an additional 385 MW of supplemental solar under the CARES 2023 program. This expansion is driven by commitments from 32 large-load customers, representing approximately 15,600 MW, and is expected to lead to annual residential savings of $102 for typical customers starting in 2029 due to a base-rate freeze.

feed.title: Verisign lifts .com wholesale fee to $10.97 as domain base hits 176.1M
feed.summary: Verisign announced its first-quarter 2026 financial results, reporting a 6.6% increase in revenue to $429 million and diluted EPS of $2.34. The company's .com and .net domain name registrations grew to 176.1 million, with a renewal rate of 75.0% for Q4 2025. Additionally, Verisign will increase the annual wholesale fee for .com domain names from $10.26 to $10.97, effective November 1, 2026.

feed.title: Verisign Reports First Quarter 2026 Results
feed.summary: Verisign reported strong financial results for the first quarter of 2026, with revenue up 6.6% year-over-year to $429 million and diluted EPS reaching $2.34. The company also saw an increase in .com and .net domain name registrations, ending the quarter with 176.1 million registrations. Furthermore, Verisign announced an upcoming increase in the annual wholesale fee for .com domain names to $10.97 effective November 1, 2026.

feed.title: Ranked: Delta Air Lines' Top 15 Long-Haul Routes [Summer 2026 Flights]
feed.summary: Delta Air Lines is preparing for its busiest long-haul summer quarter ever in Q3 2026, with 11,895 departures from the US and a 2% increase over previous records. While some hubs like Atlanta and JFK see reduced long-haul activity, others like Los Angeles, Boston, and Seattle are experiencing significant growth due to new routes. The airline's top long-haul routes include multiple daily flights to Amsterdam, London Heathrow, and Paris CDG, primarily driven by SkyTeam partnerships.

feed.title: IBM Q1 earnings beat offset by softer software momentum, says Bank of America
feed.summary: Bank of America analysts maintained a "Buy" rating on IBM following its Q1 results, which showed a modest revenue and earnings beat largely driven by infrastructure strength and lower operating expenses. Despite the beat, concerns were raised about softer organic software growth, margin compression in consulting, and limited upward revision to full-year guidance, leading to an 8% dip in shares. The analysts continue to view IBM's strategy focusing on higher-margin software and stable free cash flow as long-term supports.

feed.title: Housing Slump Hits PulteGroup Hard
feed.summary: PulteGroup (NYSE:PHM) reported a challenging first quarter with revenue declines in homebuilding and financial services due to a housing slump. While EPS missed estimates, sales exceeded expectations. The company reaffirmed its full-year outlook, anticipating improved gross margins in the second half of the year as it shifts towards more build-to-order homes.

feed.title: L3Harris sets June 26 payout for $1.25-a-share dividend
feed.summary: L3Harris Technologies (NYSE: LHX) has declared a quarterly cash dividend of $1.25 per common share. The dividend is payable on June 26, 2026, to shareholders of record as of June 5, 2026. L3Harris is described as a "Trusted Disruptor" in defense technology, providing end-to-end solutions for national security.

feed.title: Citi creates Financial and Strategic Investors group in investment banking, hires Lazard executive
feed.summary: Citigroup is establishing a new Financial and Strategic Investors group within its investment banking division to enhance transactions with financial sponsors, sovereign wealth funds, and family offices. This new group replaces the former Global Asset Management unit and will be led by Michael Marcus and Michael Quadrino in New York, and Klaus Hessberger in London, who is joining from Lazard. The move aims to boost Citi's engagement with key financial market participants.

feed.title: Charles Schwab Corp. (RBB) Rule 144 notice lists equity grant resale details
feed.summary: Charles Schwab Corp. has filed a Rule 144 notice detailing proposed sales of common stock related to equity compensation grants. The filing lists specific grant dates and corresponding share counts, with an overall neutral impact and sentiment indicated. An analyst noted it as a routine notice for proposed resales by holders, rather than new company issuances.

feed.title: Jim Cramer Shares Why Amazon.com (AMZN) Shares Are Higher
feed.summary: Jim Cramer explains that Amazon.com (AMZN) shares are higher due to its performance over the past year, recent surge in April, and a major acquisition in the satellite internet space. Cramer praised CEO Andy Jassy's shareholder letter outlining AI's role in the company's growth strategy. Amazon's stock has risen 44% in the last year and 10% year-to-date, with a 20% increase in April.

feed.title: Did Bahrain Physics-Informed AI Deal Just Shift SLB's (SLB) Investment Narrative?
feed.summary: The article discusses a partnership between Geminus AI, Bapco Energies, and SLB to implement physics-informed AI for optimizing Bahrain's upstream production system, aiming for Net-Zero by 2060. This collaboration deepens SLB's role in AI-enabled operations and digital tools within national energy networks, potentially strengthening customer ties and increasing recurring digital revenues. While showcasing SLB's digital push, the deal does not immediately alter the company's near-term earnings or ChampionX integration, with the primary risk remaining operator spending cuts.

feed.title: Did Intapp’s (INTA) Celeste–DCM Activator Integration Just Reframe Its AI-Driven Investment Narrative?
feed.summary: Intapp (INTA) has integrated DCM Insights' Activator playbooks into its Celeste AI platform, aiming to enhance business development efforts for professional and financial services firms. This move reinforces Intapp's AI-driven investment narrative, but upcoming Q3 2026 earnings and guidance will be crucial in determining whether its AI and cloud products are scaling fast enough to address market competition and its SaaS transition risks. Analysts offer a wide range of views on Intapp's future growth and profitability, highlighting the sensitivity of investor sentiment to execution.

feed.title: Booking Holdings Is Trading At A Discount After Its 16% Slump
feed.summary: Booking Holdings (NASDAQ: BKNG) has experienced a 16% year-to-date stock slump, pushing its valuation to a significant discount compared to its historical performance and competitors like Airbnb. Despite a projected slowdown in revenue growth and an antitrust investigation in Italy, the company's strong cash generation, "Connected Trip" strategy, and AI-driven efficiencies suggest it is well-positioned for a market re-evaluation. Investors are now looking to the upcoming Q1 2026 earnings report for insights into how Booking will navigate regulatory challenges and leverage its Transformation Program and stock buyback authorization.

feed.title: United Airlines Earnings: Premium Segment Should Help Weather Fuel Price Shock
feed.summary: Morningstar has slightly decreased its fair value estimate for United Airlines stock to $95 per share from $98, despite the airline's premium customer segments being expected to help weather the impact of rising fuel prices. The airline reported increased revenue and operating profit in the first quarter of 2026, and its stable of less price-sensitive customers for premium seats and business bookings is anticipated to mitigate the effect of fare increases. United has also prudently decided to pare its prior capacity growth in response to the fuel price shock.

feed.title: Food science student lands job with Kraft Heinz after global experience
feed.summary: Katie Rupert, a senior food science student at Penn State, will join Kraft Heinz Co. in Chicago for a research and development role after graduating in May. Her academic adviser, Chris Sigler, highlighted her dedication and engagement in diverse learning opportunities, including study abroad programs in Greece and Italy. Rupert emphasized how her education, including hands-on lab experience and international agriculture insights, prepared her for a career addressing global food systems and security.

feed.title: Regeneron to offer prescriptions at most favored nation prices, Trump says
feed.summary: Former U.S. President Donald Trump announced that Regeneron has agreed to offer its prescriptions at "most favored nation" prices. This move aims to potentially lower drug costs, aligning with previous efforts to reduce pharmaceutical expenditures. The announcement comes as part of broader discussions around healthcare pricing in the United States.

feed.title: Air Force seeks major increase in Lockheed JASSM missile purchases (LMT:NYSE)
feed.summary: The U.S. Air Force plans a significant increase in purchases of Lockheed Martin's JASSM cruise missiles over the next five years, as detailed in new budget documents. This expansion follows the weapon's recent use in strikes on Iranian targets and underscores its strategic importance for deterrence and potential future conflicts, particularly with China. However, the proposed procurement faces potential challenges tied to the approval of a larger defense budget by Congress.

feed.title: T-Mobile US Inc Stock (TMUS) Moved Up by 3.00% on Apr 23: Key Drivers Unveiled
feed.summary: T-Mobile US Inc (TMUS) stock rose 3.00% on April 23 due to positive analyst sentiment, anticipation of strong earnings, and speculation about a potential merger with Deutsche Telekom. Technical indicators suggest the stock may have been oversold, attracting renewed investor interest. The company outperformed the Telecommunications Services sector, which was up by 2.13%.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley (MS) sells notes linked to STOXX Europe 600 & MSCI EAFE
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley Finance LLC is offering 5-year market-linked notes with principal protected at maturity, guaranteed by Morgan Stanley. The notes' payment at maturity is linked to the worst-performing of the STOXX Europe 600 Index and the MSCI EAFE Index, offering a 142% participation rate on the upside if both indices finish above their initial levels. These structured products are intended to be treated as contingent payment debt instruments for U.S. federal income tax purposes.

feed.title: Invesco International Developed Dynamic Multifactor ETF declares quarterly distribution of $0.2250
feed.summary: The Invesco International Developed Dynamic Multifactor ETF (IMFL) has declared a quarterly distribution of $0.2250 per share. The ex-dividend date is April 25, 2026, and the payout date is April 29, 2026. This distribution reflects the fund's regular income distribution to shareholders.

feed.title: 1985 Coca-Cola Launch Goes Down in History as All-Time ‘Flop’
feed.summary: The article discusses the 1985 launch of "New Coke," which aimed to replace the original Coca-Cola formula and compete with Pepsi. Despite initial taste test preferences, widespread consumer backlash over the change led Coca-Cola to reintroduce its classic formula just 79 days later. This event is remembered as a significant marketing failure, highlighting strong consumer attachment to the original brand.

feed.title: Hannon Armstrong stock reaches 52-week high at $41.73 By Investing.com
feed.summary: Hannon Armstrong Sustainable Infrastructure Capital Inc. (HASI) stock has hit a 52-week high of $41.73, marking a 77.67% return over the past year. The company, which focuses on sustainable infrastructure investments, also offers a 4.14% dividend yield and is considered undervalued by InvestingPro. Recent financial activities include the redemption of senior notes and issuance of new green notes, alongside an increased price target from UBS.

feed.title: Herzfeld Credit Income Fund reports March NAV at $19.78
feed.summary: The Herzfeld Credit Income Fund (NYSE: RNC) announced its Net Asset Value (NAV) for March 2026 as $19.78 per share. This financial update provides investors with the latest per-share valuation of the fund's assets.

feed.title: Ikea's New Glassware Line Is 70 Percent Cheaper Than the Williams Sonoma Version—and Fans Say It Is 'Perfect'
feed.summary: Ikea has introduced its new Tjärlek glassware line, which offers a more affordable alternative to similar high-end colored glass collections, being 70% cheaper than the Williams Sonoma version. The collection features four individually mouth-blown, pastel-hued stemmed coupe glasses for $24.99 and has garnered perfect 5/5 ratings from fans. This release taps into the current trend of colored glassware, making stylish options accessible to a wider budget.

feed.title: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC Urges Gossamer Bio, Inc. Investors to Act: Class Action Filed Alleging Investor Harm
feed.summary: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC has filed a class action lawsuit against Gossamer Bio, Inc. (NASDAQ: GOSS) alleging federal securities law violations. The lawsuit claims that Gossamer Bio made misleading statements and failed to disclose material adverse facts regarding its Phase 3 PROSERA study, leading to artificially inflated stock prices. Investors who purchased Gossamer securities between June 16, 2025, and February 20, 2026, are encouraged to join the case and have until June 1, 2026, to request to be appointed as lead plaintiff.

feed.title: Texas Instruments Q1 2026 earnings beat on AI data center demand
feed.summary: Texas Instruments (TI) reported stronger-than-expected earnings for the first quarter of 2026. The company attributed this positive performance primarily to a surge in demand from the artificial intelligence (AI) data center sector. This indicates a significant impact of AI-driven growth on semiconductor manufacturers like TI.

feed.title: Union Pacific (UNP) grows Q1 2026 earnings as Norfolk Southern deal advances
feed.summary: Union Pacific (UNP) reported increased Q1 2026 earnings, with net income rising to $1.7 billion and diluted EPS to $2.87, while also advancing its planned acquisition of Norfolk Southern. Operating revenues grew 3% to $6.2 billion due to core pricing and higher fuel surcharges, despite a 1% decline in carloads, and the operating ratio improved slightly to 60.5%. The acquisition of Norfolk Southern, which involves a $20 billion cash component and approximately 225 million new shares, is still pending Surface Transportation Board approval with an expected completion in 2027.

feed.title: KeyCorp Acquisition of Clearwater Corporate Finance LLP – Deal Details and Strategy - News and Statistics
feed.summary: KeyCorp announced its definitive agreement to acquire Clearwater Corporate Finance LLP, a UK-based middle-market investment banking advisory firm. This acquisition is strategically aimed at strengthening KeyCorp's investment banking services, providing U.S. clients with access to European acquisition targets, and marking KeyCorp's first entry into the Western European market. The transaction is subject to UK regulatory approval and is expected to close in the latter half of the year.

feed.title: J.B. Hunt declares $0.45 quarterly dividend
feed.summary: J.B. Hunt Transport Services, Inc. (NASDAQ:JBHT) announced a regular quarterly dividend of $0.45 per common share, marking its 12th consecutive year of dividend increases. The dividend is payable on May 22, 2026, to stockholders of record on May 8, 2026, with a current yield of 0.73%. This declaration follows a strong first-quarter performance that exceeded analyst estimates, leading several firms to raise their price targets for the company.

feed.title: With Costco Down 7% From Its All-Time High, Could Buying Now Set You Up for Life?
feed.summary: This article questions whether investing in Costco (NASDAQ: COST) now, despite a recent 7% dip from its all-time high, would be a "set you up for life" opportunity. The author argues that its current valuation (P/E of 52.2) is extremely high, making significant future gains unlikely even for a high-quality company with consistent performance and growth plans. Instead, the article suggests adding Costco to a watchlist and waiting for a more substantial pullback before buying.

feed.title: Is It Too Late To Consider Dominion Energy (D) After Its Recent 19.6% Share Price Gain?
feed.summary: This article analyzes Dominion Energy (D) after its recent 19.6% share price increase, assessing whether it's still a good investment. Using Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) and Price-to-Earnings (P/E) ratio analyses, Simply Wall St suggests Dominion Energy is currently undervalued. The article also introduces a "Narratives" approach for investors to weigh different future scenarios for the company.

feed.title: ResMed (NYSE:RMD) Reaches New 52-Week Low - What's Next?
feed.summary: ResMed (NYSE:RMD) shares recently hit a new 52-week low of $218.03, despite favorable analyst ratings and solid financial fundamentals. The company reported strong quarterly EPS and revenue growth, maintains a low debt-to-equity ratio, and pays a consistent dividend. Analysts have a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating with an average price target of $295.17, suggesting significant upside potential.

feed.title: J.B. Hunt declares $0.45 quarterly dividend By Investing.com
feed.summary: J.B. Hunt Transport Services, Inc. has announced a regular quarterly dividend of $0.45 per common share, marking its 12th consecutive year of dividend increases. The dividend will be paid on May 22, 2026, to shareholders of record by May 8, 2026. This announcement follows a strong first-quarter earnings report where the company surpassed analyst estimates, leading to several firms raising their price targets for J.B. Hunt.

feed.title: Comcast Is Making Progress in a Tough Competitive Environment, but We Expect a Long Road to Recovery
feed.summary: Comcast is navigating a challenging competitive landscape, particularly with its core cable business facing pressure from fixed-wireless offerings and fiber expansion. While the company is making progress, Morningstar expects a long recovery period and is not optimistic about NBC and Peacock in the current media environment. However, Comcast has opportunities to strengthen NBC by leveraging unique assets like its parks business.

feed.title: Flight attendant sues Delta, says migraine leave cost him his job
feed.summary: A former Delta flight attendant is suing the airline, alleging he was fired in retaliation for taking FMLA-approved leave for a migraine. Jeremiah Harris claims Delta initiated a questionable travel audit regarding a parking receipt after he called in sick, ultimately terminating him despite his submission of supporting evidence. He is seeking reinstatement, lost wages, and damages under FMLA, ADA, and New York human rights laws.

feed.title: First Guaranty Bancshares Appoints Betsy Hood to Board
feed.summary: First Guaranty Bancshares, Inc. has appointed Betsy K. Hood to its Board of Directors, leveraging her 25 years of experience in the retail automotive industry and strong community ties. Hood's appointment aims to strengthen the bank's local connections and community-focused profile. TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, rates FGBI as Neutral, citing concerns about 2025 profitability, cash flow, and technical indicators, though corporate events such as expected capital ratio improvement offer some offset.

feed.title: AIFF excited with Genius Sports plans, clubs seek greater clarity
feed.summary: The All India Football Federation (AIFF) is enthusiastic about Genius Sports' plans to transform the Indian Super League (ISL) into a global product. Genius Sports, the preferred bidder, has offered Rs 64.4 crore annually for the commercial rights of the ISL and Federation Cup. However, ISL clubs are seeking greater clarity and more direct interaction with Genius Sports regarding their navigation of Indian football's commercial ecosystem.

feed.title: SHAREHOLDER ALERT: Levi & Korsinsky, LLP Notifies Investors of an Investigation into Forian Inc. ("FORA").
feed.summary: Levi & Korsinsky, LLP has launched an investigation into Forian Inc. (NASDAQ: FORA) concerning potential breaches of fiduciary duty by its Board of Directors. This investigation follows Forian's agreement to be acquired by an investor consortium for $2.17 per share in cash. The firm is seeking to determine if the board's actions were in the best interest of the company's stockholders.

feed.title: Details on FedEx Freight's trademark license agreement with FedEx
feed.summary: FedEx Freight will retain its brand name for five years after its spin-off from FedEx in June, under a trademark license agreement. This agreement specifies that FedEx cannot terminate the deal except under particular conditions. The article also mentions FedEx Freight's revenue target of $8.7 billion as the spin-out approaches.

feed.title: Danaher inks $173M deal to settle investor suit over post-pandemic forecast
feed.summary: Danaher has agreed to pay $173 million to settle a class-action lawsuit brought by investors. The lawsuit alleged that Danaher made misleading statements regarding its post-pandemic revenue forecasts, specifically concerning its COVID-related businesses. This settlement resolves claims that the company inflated its stock price through these projections.

feed.title: Regent Seven Seas Cruises Launches Travel Partner Council
feed.summary: Regent Seven Seas Cruises has introduced a comprehensive Travel Partner Council as part of its Regent Elevate Travel Advisor program. This initiative aims to recognize and invest in their travel advisor and agency partners through three distinct elements: the Elevate Council, the Elevate Council Elite, and the Elevate Premier Advisor. The program offers curated communications, exclusive access to events and incentives, and special recognition to foster mutual growth and collaboration.

feed.title: Fifth Third explores 'multiple options' for main office as bank begins branch expansion
feed.summary: Fifth Third Bank is exploring options for a major main office in Dallas while simultaneously initiating a significant branch expansion. This move comes after its nearly $11 billion acquisition of Comerica, signaling a strong focus on growth in the North Texas market.

feed.title: Gartner: Global IT spending to grow by 13.5% this year
feed.summary: Gartner forecasts global IT spending to reach $6.31 trillion this year, a 13.5% increase from 2025, primarily driven by investments in AI. The data center systems segment is expected to see the fastest growth at 55.8%, while IT services will remain the largest spending category exceeding $1.87 trillion. Software, especially generative AI, also shows strong growth, with device market growth slowing due to rising memory prices.

feed.title: Wasatch Advisors (NASDAQ: GSHD) reports 2.29M-share stake, 9.7% ownership
feed.summary: Wasatch Advisors has reported a beneficial ownership of 2,292,227 shares, equating to 9.7% of Goosehead Insurance Inc. Class A stock, through an Amendment No. 8 to a Schedule 13G/A filing. The filing indicates Wasatch has sole voting power for 1,513,266 shares and sole dispositive power for all 2,292,227 shares. This is a passive institutional ownership disclosure, not signaling an intent to influence management.

feed.title: EXEC: Hydro Flask Tops Plan, Osprey Grows in Helen of Troy’s Fiscal Q4
feed.summary: Helen of Troy's Home & Outdoor segment experienced a modest decline in fiscal Q4, but Hydro Flask exceeded expectations due to new product launches, and Osprey's sales grew despite overall category challenges. OXO also performed better than anticipated. The company is strategically expanding product lines and leveraging e-commerce and new innovations to drive growth across these key brands.

feed.title: FuboTV to bring Multiview feature to LG TVs for football season By Investing.com
feed.summary: FuboTV announced it is developing its user-configured Multiview feature for LG TVs, aiming to launch it before the 2026 football season. This will allow users to stream up to four live channels simultaneously, a feature already available on Apple TV and select Roku devices. FuboTV expects to be the first virtual MVPD to offer this customizable Multiview experience on LG TVs, including 2024, 2025, and newer 4K and 8K models.
